  - "If your organization relies on WPA2/3 encryption for its wireless networks, you need to assess your exposure to AirSnitch. Review your network segmentation and investigate any tools or configurations that might be susceptible to bypassing client isolation."

---

Palo Alto Unit 42 has identified a new threat dubbed 'AirSnitch' that circumvents modern Wi-Fi encryption standards like WPA2 and WPA3. This attack also bypasses client isolation, a crucial security feature designed to prevent devices on the same network from communicating with each other. The implications for enterprises are significant, as it opens the door to unauthorized access and potential compromise of sensitive internal systems.
